title: Open Problem (linear-time 3-coloring output)
statement_text: Does there exist a linear-time algorithm to output a 3-coloring of a triangle-free graph embedded in a fixed surface (with a bounded number of precolored boundary vertices), using ideas similar to those of Theorem 1.3?
context_text: Theorem 1.4 gives a quadratic-time algorithm to output a 3-coloring in the affirmative case. The authors believe a linear-time algorithm for the output step exists via ideas analogous to those of the linear-time decision algorithm (Theorem 1.3), but note significant technical challenges in realising it.
notes: Stated in running prose in the introduction: 'we believe that there exists a linear-time algorithm to output a 3-coloring using ideas similar to those of Theorem 1.3; however, there are significant technical challenges in designing it and we leave this as an open problem.' No labelled theorem environment.
paper_title: Three-coloring triangle-free graphs on surfaces VII. A linear-time algorithm
